contexts: Pre-war close — baseline
Day 3 — First trading session; S&P recovers from -1.2% intraday to flat
Day 4 — S&P -0.94%; Brent +4.71%; Hormuz closure formal
Day 5 — S&P bounces +0.78%; oil prices steady
Day 6 — Pentagon: $11.3B cumulative cost; S&P -0.56%
Day 7 — S&P -1.33%; WTI best week since 1983; worst S&P week in 5 months
Day 10 — Brent intraday $119.50; Trump says war "very complete"
Day 11 — Brent crashes 11.3% on false Hormuz escort report
Day 12 — IEA 400M bbl reserve release; CSIS: $16.5B cumulative
Day 13 — Brent closes above $100; S&P -1.52% (worst since Feb)
Day 14 — S&P hits 2026 low at 6,632; Brent $103.14; AUMF debate
Day 17 — Brent $106.18 on Fujairah/Dubai attacks; S&P futures +0.5%
Day 18 — Brent pulls back to $101; S&P recovers to 6,735; Ford fire contained
Day 19 — South Pars struck; Brent $108.43 (+52.7%); IRGC threatens Gulf energy; 3 officials killed
Day 20 — Iran retaliates on Gulf energy; Brent intraday $119.11; S&P 6,618 new 2026 low; Dow -768; Fed holds 3.50-3.75%
Day 21 — Quadruple Witching; S&P 6,506.48 new 2026 low; 3 consecutive weekly losses; Brent $112.19 (Fri close); Nasdaq -2.01%; Goldman 7,600 / JPMorgan 7,200

Day 24 — Trump postpones power plant strikes 5 days; Brent crashes 13.6% to $96.91 then recovers to ~$104; S&P surges ~1.7-2.2% to ~6,628; Dow +960; gold -6.3%; Asian markets did not benefit; defense/energy stocks fall
Day 25 — Oil rebounds 3-4% as Iran denies talks; S&P ~flat; defense -1.5%; energy outperforms; Pakistan emerges as mediator; Tripoli enters CENTCOM Friday

Day 26 — 15-point ceasefire plan; Brent -3.5% (largest drop of war) to $96.68; S&P +0.6% to ~6,596; all sectors green except energy; ARM +15%; gold rebounds +2.1% to $4,560; Iran rejects plan as 'maximalist'

Day 27 — Iran definitively rejects ceasefire; Israel kills IRGC Navy Cmdr Tangsiri; Brent surges to $105.85 (+3.8-6.4%); S&P ~6,550; gold drops to ~$4,429 (-2.4-2.9%); AP: 'stocks fall'; defense RTX +22.1% MTD; Carnival -27.4% MTD

Day 28 — Houthi missile barrage at Israel opens third front; WTI briefly crosses $100; Brent $112.57 (+4.22%); S&P 6,369 (-1.67%); Dow enters correction; VIX 31.05; fifth straight weekly decline; CNN 'extreme fear'; XOM new 52-wk high +39% YTD; gold $4,524

Day 31 — Mon open: S&P futures +0.7% on Powell 'well anchored' inflation remarks; Brent $114.90-$116+ intraday; WTI $101-103; Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran infrastructure; Islamabad summit outcome; defense stocks not sustaining surge despite combat; avg S&P member -17% from highs; avg Nasdaq member -31%

Day 32 — Iranian drone strikes Kuwaiti VLCC Al Salmi at Port of Dubai; Brent $112.78 settle nearing $120 on attack; gas crosses $4.00 nationally; 10Y extreme volatility 4.31-4.43%; Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure threatened — dual chokepoint; gold $4,578-4,600; Moody's recession prob 49%; XOM +28% YTD $171; CVX $210.71 near 52-wk high; DAL $64 UAL $91 down 15-20% monthly

Day 33 — Trump claims Iran asked for CEASEFIRE; markets surge on peace hopes; S&P 6,528.52 (+2.91% best day since May); Dow +2.49%; Nasdaq +3.83%; Brent drops 5% to ~$105 intraday; VIX collapses 17.5% to 25.25; Asian markets explode: Kospi +8.4%, Nikkei +5.2%; gold $4,746 (+1.9%); 10Y 4.31%; RTX $192.90 (+3.1%); LMT ~$610; airlines rally DAL $62.89 UAL $92.26; Apr 6 energy deadline 5 days out; Trump prime-time address 9 PM ET tonight; Rubio: 'we can see the finish line'

Day 34 — S&P 6,582.69 (+0.11%) — dramatic intraday reversal from post-Trump speech selloff on Iran-Oma transit protocol headlines; first weekly gain since conflict began (+3.4%); Dow 46,504.67 (-0.13%); Nasdaq 21,879.18 (+0.18%); VIX 23.87 (-2.73%); Brent $109.03 (+7.8%); WTI $111.54 (+11.4%, first close above $100 since Jul 2022); WTI-Brent spread inverted; gold $4,692.80 (-2.50%); 10Y 4.31% (-0.6 bps); XOM $160.69 (-5.2%); CVX $197.41 (-4.6%); DAL $58.78; UAL $91-95 (5% capacity cut); LMT $622.89 (+30% YTD); RTX $193-197; Apr 3 markets closed Good Friday; Apr 6 deadline 3 days; war approval 28% YouGov

Day 38 — S&P 6,611.83 (+0.44%) — four consecutive up sessions (+3.3% for week); Dow 46,669.88 (+0.36%); Nasdaq 21,996.34 (+0.54%); VIX 24.17 (+1.26%); Brent $111.25; WTI $111.81; gold $4,672-4,700; 10Y 4.335%; LMT $622.79 (+26% YTD); RTX $198.41 (+40% YoY); KTOS $74.09 (+10.07%); XOM ~$163.37 (+28-41% YTD); CVX $198.86 (+24% YTD); DAL $64-65; UAL ~$92; AAL ~$11 (-25-30% YTD); Easter Monday open (not a US holiday); 8 PM ET deadline tomorrow

Day 39 — S&P opened ~6,588 under pressure; range 6,579-6,618; down ~0.5-0.7% on Kharg Island strikes and escalating rhetoric; VIX 24.88 (+4.2%); WTI spiking toward $115 on Kharg strikes (50+ targets); Brent $109.87-111.68; gold $4,672-4,700; 10Y 4.323-4.353%; Goldman warns S&P could hit 5,400 on persistent disruptions; Kalshi 60% prob S&P below 5,900 in 2026; IRGC threatens strikes 'outside the region'; Vance: 'military goals achieved'

Day 40 — CEASEFIRE: Pakistan-brokered two-week pause announced 6:32 PM ET Apr 7 — 88 min before deadline; S&P ~6,609; Dow futures +1,000+ pts overnight; Brent crashes 14-16% to ~$93; WTI plunges 15-19% to ~$96 (one of largest single-day drops in history); gold $4,737 (still rising — market skepticism); 10Y yields plummeting; VIX expected sharp decline; XOM -6% to ~$154; CVX -5.5% to ~$190; airlines expected +9-14%; defense faces ceasefire headwind; UBS cuts S&P target 7,700→7,500; 'death cross' formed; Islamabad talks Apr 10
